# fly_postgres_cluster (Resource)

Manages a Fly.io Postgres cluster. Import using the cluster name.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "fly_postgres_cluster" "example" {
  name           = "my-pg-cluster"
  org            = "personal"
  region         = "iad"
  cluster_size   = 1
  volume_size    = 10
  vm_size        = "shared-cpu-1x"
  enable_backups = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The name of the Postgres cluster. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `org` (String) The organization slug. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `region` (String) The primary region. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Optional

- `cluster_size` (Number) The number of nodes in the cluster. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `enable_backups` (Boolean) Enable automatic backups. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `vm_size` (String) The VM size (e.g., shared-cpu-1x). Changing this forces a new resource.
- `volume_size` (Number) Volume size in GB. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the Postgres cluster.
- `status` (String) The cluster status.
